title: Ajanta Pharma Promoter Entity Sells 2.8% Stake in Rs 1,024 Crore Bulk Deal
summary: Bengaluru: A promoter entity of Ajanta Pharma sold shares worth USD 107.4 million (Rs 10.24 billion) via bulk deal on Tuesday, according to exchange data.‌Ravi Agrawal ⁠Trust, ⁠a promoter group entity...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Bengaluru: A promoter entity of Ajanta Pharma sold shares worth USD 107.4 million (Rs 10.24 billion) via bulk deal on Tuesday, according to exchange data.

‌Ravi Agrawal ⁠Trust, ⁠a promoter group entity of the drugmaker, ​sold 3.5 million shares, representing about 2.8% stake, ​according to bulk deal data from the National Stock Exchange of ​India

Shares were sold ⁠at 2,968 ‌rupees each, which is ​at ​a 0.03% discount to Monday's ⁠closing price

Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund and Aditya Birla Sun Life Mutual Fund bought 2.1 million and 1.3 million shares of Ajanta Pharma, respectively

Ravi Agrawal Trust held 16.2 million shares in the firm, ‌equivalent to a 13% stake, according to exchange data as of March-end

In ⁠December 2022, promoters Ravi Agrawal Trust and Aayush Agrawal Trust had together sold about ​4.6% stake in Ajanta Pharma via a block deal, according to an Economic Times report.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
